scope: Independent verification of VS Code and Obsidian thin clients, shared protocol client artifacts, tests, cancellation/debounce behavior, provider boundary, and MVP usability gaps.

summary.result: VS Code and Obsidian client artifacts are trusted for the thin-client source/test slice. Relevant tests and static checks pass, generated client protocol artifacts match the Rust protocol exporter, and no blocking source-level provider-boundary or protocol-shape defects were found.
non_blocking_notes: Ghost text and Tab acceptance are verified by Node host-glue tests, not by real VS Code or Obsidian extension-host smoke. This remains an MVP acceptance evidence gap for MVP-050/final verification, not a source-level failure in this slice.

Both editor clients import shared protocol helpers by repository-relative paths. That is adequate for current local-source tests, but packaged VS Code/Obsidian distribution needs a bundling or copy step so the protocol helper files are present.

Obsidian runtime module resolution is not independently proven: src/main.js statically parses, but it requires Obsidian and CodeMirror modules in an actual Obsidian plugin environment. A disposable-vault smoke should verify this before claiming UI acceptance.
Client UX for broker failures is intentionally minimal: network/HTTP errors are swallowed into no suggestion, with no user-visible diagnostics or health indicator.

Settings are read when providers/controllers are created. Existing VS Code providers and Obsidian editor views may need reload/reopen to pick up debounce/deadline/context changes reliably. This is acceptable for MVP but should be documented.

commands_run.result: passed. 9 Node tests cover shared protocol request shape, /v1/autocomplete endpoint use, stale cancellation, VS Code request construction/rendering, Obsidian request construction/acceptance, and provider-boundary scanning.

commands_run.command: node --check clients/obsidian/src/main.js && node --check clients/obsidian/src/plugin-core.js && node --check clients/vscode/src/extension.js && node --check clients/vscode/src/extension-core.js && node --check clients/protocol/src/client.js && node --check clients/protocol/src/generated/protocol-v1.js
cargo run -q -p autocomplete-protocol --bin export_client_artifacts -- <temp-dir>; diff -ru clients/protocol/src/generated <temp-dir>
commands_run.result: passed. Generated JS protocol constants and JSON Schemas are in sync with the Rust protocol exporter.
passed. Full Rust workspace tests passed, including protocol schema/fixture validation, server cancellation/reload/privacy behavior, provider-pi isolation tests, and postprocessing tests.
passed. Only the boundary test itself contains split forbidden-token strings; production client files contain no provider-pi imports or provider-specific logic.

evidence: clients/vscode/src/extension.js imports only vscode runtime APIs, clients/protocol BrokerClient, and VS Code core glue.
clients/obsidian/src/main.js imports only Obsidian/CodeMirror runtime APIs, clients/protocol BrokerClient and trigger constants, and Obsidian core glue.
clients/vscode/src/extension-core.js and clients/obsidian/src/plugin-core.js both call shared clients/protocol helpers; neither imports Rust crates or provider-pi internals.
clients/protocol/test/boundaries.test.js enforces absence of provider-pi/provider-pi-style symbols across client JS/JSON artifacts.

Both clients construct requests through clients/protocol/src/client.js:createAutocompleteRequest, which emits protocol_version, request_id, client, document, cursor, context, and options matching protocol v1.

VS Code fills client.name=vscode, document.uri/language_id/version from the VS Code TextDocument, cursor line/character/offset from the Position, and options mode=inline_tab plus max_chars/deadline_ms/trigger.
Obsidian fills client.name=obsidian, markdown language_id, obsidian:// document URI, cursor line/character/offset from CodeMirror state, and the same options fields.
The generated protocol-v1.js constants and request/response JSON Schemas exactly match a fresh export from crates/autocomplete-protocol.
BrokerClient.autocomplete posts JSON to the generated AUTOCOMPLETE_PATH (/v1/autocomplete).
BrokerClient.cancel posts to the generated CANCEL_PATH_PREFIX (/v1/cancel/:request_id).
Provider selection, prompting, postprocessing, privacy policy, and pi subprocess logic remain in Rust broker/provider crates and are not referenced by clients.

BrokerAutocompleteSession cancels the current pending request before accepting a replacement, aborts local fetch via AbortController, calls broker cancel for the stale request_id, and resolves stale completions as null.
clients/protocol/test/client.test.js verifies a stale in-flight request is cancelled and its late response ignored.
VS Code provider uses BrokerAutocompleteSession and adapts VS Code cancellation tokens to AbortSignal; extension-core tests verify replacement cancellation before rendering fresh ghost text.

Obsidian controller uses BrokerAutocompleteSession, increments a local documentVersion, clears suggestions on state changes, and only stores suggestions matching the current version; plugin-core tests verify stale cancellation and Tab acceptance.
VS Code provider returns vscode.InlineCompletionItem(insertText, range) for fresh ok responses, which is the editor's inline ghost-text path.
Obsidian main.js creates CodeMirror widget decorations for suggestion text and a high-priority Tab keymap that accepts only when a current suggestion is present.
Tests verify VS Code item construction and Obsidian controller insertion transaction on Tab acceptance.
ghost_text_and_acceptance.remaining_evidence_gap: No real editor-host smoke was configured or run in this verification task.

strengths: Tests exercise protocol shape and host glue with fake editor/runtime objects rather than only shallow import checks.
Cancellation is tested at shared session level and through both editor cores.
Boundary scanning is automated to catch provider-pi leakage into clients.
Rust protocol/server/provider tests still pass, reducing risk that client protocol artifacts drifted from broker behavior.
gaps_not_blocking: No VS Code Extension Development Host smoke test.
No disposable Obsidian vault/plugin load smoke test.
No packaging/bundling test proving repo-relative shared protocol imports work after installation.
No explicit test for VS Code token cancellation without a replacement request, although the adapter code is present.

limitations_for_mvp_acceptance: Actual UI display and Tab acceptance remain to be proven in real editors.
Packaging/build instructions are minimal, especially for Obsidian CodeMirror dependencies and shared protocol helper inclusion.
No visible broker health/error UX exists yet; broker downtime appears as absent suggestions.
Dynamic settings reload is limited for already-created providers/controllers.
Autocomplete quality/latency with real pi remains explicitly unmeasured and should remain in MVP-050.

queue_recommendation: Proceed to MVP-050 runtime smoke and measurement. Focus that slice on proving real VS Code and Obsidian loading/rendering, validating packaging/module resolution assumptions, and recording pi/mock latency before final MVP acceptance.
